items.headline: 4th straight 1st-round exit leaves the Lightning searching for answers
items.description:
TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — The Tampa Bay Lightning dominated most of Game 7, had triple the scoring opportunities and still found a way to lose.
It’s an early exit for the fourth straight season.

items.headline: Maple Leafs hire Sundin, Chayka to lead front office
items.description:
TORONTO (AP) — The Toronto Maple Leafs hired former captain Mats Sundin as senior executive adviser of hockey operations and John Chayka as general manager Sunday.
The moves mark a reset of the club’s front office after a season that ended with Toronto missing the playoffs for the first time since 2016.

items.headline: Tocchet looks for Flyers to 'figure it out quickly' entering Game 2 against Hurricanes
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — It was a rough start for the Philadelphia Flyers in the second round of the NHL playoffs. Coach Rick Tocchet knows his team has to adjust quickly against the Carolina Hurricanes as the top seed in the Eastern Conference.
The Hurricanes won 3-0 in Saturday night's best-of-seven opener, with Tocchet saying his team didn't react quickly enough to the Hurricanes' aggressive skating that ultimately smothered the Flyers' attack. Game 2 is Monday night.

items.headline: Man disadvantage in Buffalo. Sabres advance to 2nd round despite anemic power-play production
items.description:
BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — Call it the man disadvantage in Buffalo.
Sabres captain Rasmus Dahlin can appreciate how much room for improvement there is for a power-play unit that produced at one of the worst rates in an NHL playoff series over the past 50 years.

items.headline: Wild will be without Joel Eriksson Ek and Jonas Brodin for first two games of series vs. Avs
items.description:
DENVER (AP) — The Minnesota Wild will be without forward Joel Eriksson Ek and defenseman Jonas Brodin for the first two games of their second-round series against the Colorado Avalanche because of lower-body injuries.
Neither player made the trip to Denver. Wild coach John Hynes said the team is still “making a couple decisions on the lineup" ahead of Game 1 on Sunday night.

items.headline: Flyers' Tippett, Hurricanes' Nikishin out for opener of 2nd-round series
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Philadelphia forward Owen Tippett sat out the Flyers’ 3-0 loss to the Carolina Hurricanes on Saturday night in Game 1 of the second-round playoff series.
The Flyers ruled out their regular-season goals leader about a half-hour before the start of the game due to an undisclosed injury.

items.headline: Jackson LaCombe leads his Anaheim Ducks into the 2nd round with a phenomenal playoff debut
items.description:
ANAHEIM, Calif. (AP) — When Jackson LaCombe signed a $72 million contract extension with the Anaheim Ducks last September, casual hockey fans around North America responded to the news online with countless variations of the same clueless refrain: “Who's that?”
Seven months later, anyone who still doesn't know about the Ducks' top defenseman clearly isn't paying attention to this sport.

items.headline: Oilers need some new players after early playoff ouster, GM Stan Bowman says
items.description:
EDMONTON, Alberta (AP) — Saying Saturday was not a day for big decisions or announcements, Edmonton Oilers general manager Stan Bowman did concede that next season’s team will “not be the same group as today.”
Two days after the Oilers were eliminated in six games in the first round of the NHL playoffs, Bowman told reporters there are positives in young players like Matt Savoie, Josh Samanski and Vasily Podkolzin, but changes to the roster are definitely needed.

items.headline: Canadiens-Lightning series comes down to Game 7
items.description:
TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Six one-goal games. Four overtime games. Now, Game 7.
It’s a fitting way to decide the Canadiens-Lightning series on Sunday night.

items.headline: Golden Knights build on late-season success under coach John Tortorella in playoffs
items.description:
SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — John Tortorella and the Vegas Golden Knights are off to the second round of the NHL playoffs after pushing aside the pesky Utah Mammoth.
Vegas ground out three straight victories to finish off the Mammoth, winning 5-1 on Friday night in Game 6 after consecutive overtime wins. Vegas overcame third-period deficits in its first three victories.
